reason: hsCRP assays report values 0.1-3 mg/L; setting floor at 0.1 prevents rejection of real hsCRP results while still catching zero/negative entry errors
Unit-confusion tightening caps at ~18 but CRP 50-300+ mg/L is real in severe sepsis; the mg/dL-as-mg/L confusion only produces falsely LOW values so high-end tightening is counterproductive
